visual_checks: Check if a non-conforming version of the document exists alongside a conforming alternate version
Verify that the conforming alternate version contains the same information and functionality as the primary version
Confirm the alternate version is equally current and up to date with the primary version
Look for a clear mechanism to reach the conforming alternate version from the non-conforming version
Verify that the conforming alternate version is in an accessible format and meets applicable accessibility requirements
Check that the path from the non-conforming version to the conforming alternate is itself accessible
pass_conditions: A conforming alternate version is provided that contains the same information as the non-conforming version
The alternate version is equally current and updated whenever the primary version changes
The conforming alternate version is reachable directly from the non-conforming version via an accessible mechanism
The alternate version meets all applicable WCAG success criteria
The conforming version provides equivalent functionality and information to the non-conforming version
fail_conditions: A non-conforming document is published with no conforming alternate version available
The alternate version exists but is outdated compared to the primary version
The alternate version is not reachable from the non-conforming version (no link, reference, or accessible path)
The alternate version is missing content or information that is present in the primary version
The mechanism to reach the alternate version is itself not accessible (e.g., link is not perceivable or operable)
The alternate version exists but also fails to meet accessibility requirements
The alternate version is in a format that is less usable than necessary (e.g., plain text dump of a complex interactive document)
na_conditions: Only one version of the document exists and no alternate version is needed
The primary document itself is fully conforming and no alternate version is required
ne_conditions: It could not be determined whether an alternate version exists
The relationship between document versions could not be evaluated from the available data
common_mistakes: Providing an alternate version that was created from an earlier draft and not updated with the final content
Offering an HTML alternate of a PDF but omitting tables, images, or sections from the conversion
Placing the conforming alternate version on a different site or location with no link from the non-conforming version
Providing a text-only version as the alternate but stripping out meaningful formatting, structure, and media
Relying on users to find the alternate version through search rather than providing a direct accessible path
Creating an accessible version once and not maintaining it as the primary document is updated

examples.pass: A scanned PDF (non-conforming) includes a direct link on the download page to a tagged, accessible PDF version that contains the same content and was published on the same date
examples.fail: A complex infographic PDF is posted with a note saying 'accessible version available upon request' but no direct link to an existing alternate version is provided
examples.partial: An accessible HTML version of a PDF report is linked from the download page, but the HTML version is missing two appendices that are present in the PDF

plain_meaning: When a piece of web content cannot be made directly accessible, a Conforming Alternate Version must be provided: a separate version that meets WCAG at the required level, contains equivalent information and functionality, is kept as up to date as the primary version, and is reachable from the non-conforming version via an accessible mechanism. The alternate is not a second-class fallback; it must be equivalent in content and current in updates.
scope_applies_to: Non-conforming primary content (for example scanned PDFs, complex infographics) that ships with an accessible alternate
The mechanism on the non-conforming version that points to the alternate
The equivalence of information and functionality between versions
The currency and update schedule of the alternate vs the primary
The accessibility of the path from primary to alternate
The conformance of the alternate itself to WCAG at the required level
scope_does_not_apply_to: Content that is itself fully conforming (no alternate required)
Sites with a single version where no alternate version is needed
Alternate versions that exist only as internal drafts and are not published
Accessibility concerns that belong to other specific SCs when applied to the alternate version
Search-only discoverability when a direct link is required
borderline_calibration: An alternate that is missing content the primary has is a FAIL.
An alternate that is out of date relative to the primary is a FAIL.
An alternate that cannot be reached via an accessible link from the non-conforming version is a FAIL.
A 'request an accessible copy' note with no pre-existing alternate does NOT satisfy this criterion.
If the primary is already conforming, return Not Applicable rather than PASS.
